Document common installation problems including dependency conflicts, build failures, and platform-specific issues. Explain debugging techniques for authentication problems, game launch failures, and desktop integration issues. Include troubleshooting steps for performance problems, memory leaks, and system resource usage. Document error codes, log analysis, and diagnostic procedures for different components. Provide solutions for network connectivity issues, firewall configuration, and proxy settings. Include FAQ sections covering user questions about features, pricing, and account management. Address recovery procedures for corrupted installations and data restoration. Provide escalation procedures for complex issues and support channels.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# SBGames Project Architecture Overview

## 1. Project Introduction

**SBGames** is a comprehensive Minecraft launcher and gaming platform designed for the SB Games ecosystem. The project serves as both a desktop application and web-based platform, providing users with seamless access to Minecraft gameplay, social features, and monetization systems.

### Purpose Statement
- **Primary Goal**: Deliver a secure, feature-rich Minecraft launcher with integrated social networking, trading, and monetization capabilities
- **Target Audience**: Minecraft players seeking an all-in-one gaming experience with social features and virtual economy
- **Core Value Proposition**: Unified platform combining game launching, social interaction, marketplace functionality, and administrative tools

### Key Objectives
- Secure game launching with integrity verification
- Real-time social networking and communication
- Virtual currency system with marketplace integration
- Cross-platform desktop application with native OS integration
- Comprehensive administrative dashboard

## 2. Technical Architecture

The SBGames project follows a modern, multi-layered architecture combining desktop application development with web technologies and backend services.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[SBGames Launcher] --> B[Tauri Desktop Framework]
    A --> C[React Frontend]
    B --> D[Rust Backend Services]
    C --> E[Web APIs]
    E --> F[Node.js Authentication Server]
    F --> G[Redis Cache]
    F --> H[MongoDB/MySQL]
    I[Website Platform] --> J[Vite Build System]
    J --> K[Static React Application]
    L[Java Bootstrap] --> M[Forge Modpack Loader]
    N[Mobile/Web Clients] --> O[RESTful API Gateway]
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Layer**
- **React-based Launcher**: Modern desktop application with smooth animations and responsive design
- **Website Platform**: Separate web interface for non-desktop users and administrative functions
- **Real-time Communication**: WebSocket-based live updates and notifications

**Backend Layer**
- **Authentication Server**: Node.js-based REST API with WebSocket support
- **Security Services**: Java-based bootstrap loader with integrity verification
- **Data Management**: Redis caching layer with database persistence

**Native Integration**
- **Tauri Framework**: Rust-based desktop application with native OS capabilities
- **System Integration**: Windows tray integration, Discord Rich Presence, desktop notifications

### Design Patterns

The project implements several key architectural patterns:

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class App {
        +useState()
        +handleLogin()
        +handleLogout()
    }
    class MainLayout {
        +useEffect()
        +WebSocketConnection()
        +NavigationSystem()
    }
    class AuthServer {
        +JWTAuthentication()
        +RateLimiting()
        +RedisIntegration()
    }
    class BootstrapLoader {
        +IntegrityVerification()
        +SecurityMonitoring()
        +ModpackValidation()
    }
    
    App --> MainLayout
    MainLayout --> AuthServer
    BootstrapLoader --> ForgeLauncher
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Desktop Application Entry**
- `src/main.jsx` - React application root with strict mode rendering
- `src/App.jsx` - Root component managing authentication state and routing
- `src/tray.jsx` - Tray popup application for system tray interactions

**Backend Services**
- `server/index.js` - Primary authentication and API server with WebSocket support
- `src-tauri/src/main.rs` - Tauri application entry point with window management
- `src-tauri/src/lib.rs` - Rust library exports for JavaScript interop

### Core Modules

**Authentication & Security**
- `src/lib/api.js` - Centralized API communication with token management
- `src/lib/tauri.js` - Tauri integration layer for native functionality
- `src-java/com/sbgames/bootstrap/SBGBootstrap.java` - Java-based security verification

**Component Architecture**
- `src/pages/MainLayout.jsx` - Primary navigation and content management
- `src/components/` - Reusable UI components with custom cursor and notification systems
- `src/hooks/` - Custom React hooks for state management and effects

### Configuration Approach

**Build Configuration**
- `package.json` - Main project configuration with build scripts and dependencies
- `vite.config.js` - Vite build system configuration for both frontend and tray builds
- `src-tauri/Cargo.toml` - Rust crate dependencies and Tauri configuration
- `src-tauri/tauri.conf.json` - Desktop application configuration including CSP and window management

**Environment Management**
- Multi-environment setup supporting development, staging, and production
- Secure credential handling with environment variable management
- CORS configuration for cross-origin resource sharing

### External Dependencies

**Frontend Dependencies**
- `@tauri-apps/api` - Desktop application integration
- `framer-motion` - Animation library for smooth transitions
- `react-router-dom` - Client-side routing
- `axios` - HTTP client for API communication

**Backend Dependencies**
- `express` - Web server framework
- `ws` - WebSocket implementation
- `ioredis` - Redis client for caching
- `jsonwebtoken` - JWT token generation and verification

**Desktop Integration**
- `discord-rich-presence` - Discord integration for status display
- `pngjs` - PNG image processing
- `ssh2` - SSH connectivity for server management

### Integration Points

**API Integration**
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client as SBGames Launcher
    participant AuthServer as Authentication Server
    participant Redis as Redis Cache
    participant DB as Database
    
    Client->>+AuthServer: Login Request
    AuthServer->>+Redis: Check Credentials
    Redis-->>-AuthServer: Cache Response
    AuthServer->>+DB: Validate User
    DB-->>-AuthServer: User Data
    AuthServer-->>-Client: JWT Token + User Data
    
    Note over Client,AuthServer: Real-time Updates via WebSocket
    Client->>+AuthServer: WebSocket Connection
    AuthServer-->>-Client: Live Notifications
```

**Desktop Integration**
- Windows tray integration with custom popup interface
- Discord Rich Presence for real-time status display
- System notification system with desktop alerts
- Minecraft process monitoring and control

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Game Launching & Management**
- Secure modpack verification and integrity checking
- Automatic game updates and version management
- Process monitoring and crash recovery
- Performance optimization and resource management

**Social Networking System**
- Real-time messaging and friend system
- Group creation and management
- Online presence indicators
- Activity tracking and leaderboards

**Economic System**
- Virtual currency (SBT) transactions
- Marketplace for player-to-player trading
- Item shop with cosmetic enhancements
- Balance management and transaction history

**Administrative Tools**
- User management and moderation
- Ticket system for support requests
- Content moderation and reporting
- Analytics and usage statistics

### Implementation Highlights

**Security Implementation**
- Java-based bootstrap loader with XOR-encoded strings
- Integrity verification through SHA-256 hashing
- Debugger detection and protection mechanisms
- Secure token-based authentication

**Performance Optimization**
- Redis caching for frequently accessed data
- WebSocket-based real-time communication
- Lazy loading for optimal resource usage
- Efficient state management across components

**Cross-Platform Compatibility**
- Tauri framework for native desktop applications
- Responsive web design for multiple screen sizes
- Platform-specific optimizations and integrations
- Consistent user experience across devices

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Unauthenticated
    Unauthenticated --> Authenticating: Login Attempt
    Authenticating --> Authenticated: Success
    Authenticating --> Unauthenticated: Failure
    Authenticated --> GameSelection: Select Game
    GameSelection --> Launching: Start Game
    Launching --> Playing: Game Running
    Playing --> GameSelection: Exit Game
    Authenticated --> ProfileManagement: Manage Profile
    Authenticated --> SocialFeatures: Chat/Friends
    Authenticated --> Marketplace: Buy/Sell Items
    Authenticated --> Support: Submit Tickets
    ProfileManagement --> Authenticated: Back to Home
    SocialFeatures --> Authenticated: Back to Home
    Marketplace --> Authenticated: Back to Home
    Support --> Authenticated: Back to Home
```

**Notification System**
- Desktop notifications with custom positioning
- In-app notification center with filtering
- Real-time updates via WebSocket connections
- Persistent notification preferences

**Tray Integration**
- Minimized application with tray-only visibility
- Context menu with quick actions
- Live status updates and notifications
- System tray icon with dynamic states

The SBGames project represents a sophisticated gaming platform that successfully combines modern web technologies with native desktop application capabilities, creating a comprehensive gaming ecosystem with robust security, real-time communication, and economic systems.

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [Cargo.toml](src-tauri/Cargo.toml)
- [tauri.conf.json](src-tauri/tauri.conf.json)
- [App.jsx](src/App.jsx)
- [main.jsx](src/main.jsx)
- [tray.jsx](src/tray.jsx)
- [server package.json](server/package.json)
- [server index.js](server/index.js)
- [website package.json](website/package.json)
- [website App.jsx](website/src/App.jsx)
- [MainLayout.jsx](src/pages/MainLayout.jsx)
- [SBGBootstrap.java](src-java/com/sbgames/bootstrap/SBGBootstrap.java)
- [api.js](src/lib/api.js)
- [tauri.js](src/lib/tauri.js)

</blog>
